text.title: AI Pair-Coding Prompt Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every ai-pair-coding-prompt-patterns artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every prompt-scaffold MUST name one of {spec-first, test-first, repo-grep, diff-review, refactor-isolate, plan-then-execute, branch-canary}. Custom / unnamed patterns are rejected.
rule.rationale: Naming the pattern constrains the stop conditions; ad-hoc prompts produce drifting diffs.

rule.statement: Scaffold MUST include >= 1 explicit stop condition (e.g. "stop when tests pass", "stop when file is >100 LOC"). Open-ended prompts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a stop condition the AI keeps editing until it hits a model-token wall; the diff sprawls.

rule.statement: Scaffold MUST include an acceptance_checklist with >= 3 binary items the AI's output is graded against.
rule.rationale: Checklist gives the reviewer (or the AI's own self-review) a concrete pass/fail signal.

rule.statement: For tasks involving existing code, scaffold MUST require a repo-grep step before inventing imports / functions. Pure-invention prompts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: AI hallucinates imports of functions that look like they should exist; the grep step grounds the prompt in reality.

rule.statement: Each scaffold MUST address one semantic unit. Bundling feature + refactor + fix into a single scaffold is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bundled prompts produce bundled diffs; rollback becomes impossible.
